project.name: Module 12: Popup UI — Settings & Utilities
project.description: Create SettingsScreen with password change, recovery phrase verification, import/export (Chrome bookmarks, HTML file, encrypted backup), password set management, auto-lock config, theme toggle, and clear data. CRITICAL: This module also wires up the 10 NOT_IMPLEMENTED message handlers in background.ts. Split into SETTINGS-001a (backend handlers) and SETTINGS-001b (UI) for independent testability. Target: SettingsScreen ≤300 lines, each sub-component ≤200 lines.

preserve_verbatim: Chrome bookmark import flow (click → chrome.bookmarks.getTree → convert → save)

falls_short.hush_requires: Password change: current → new → confirm → re-encrypt all data.

falls_short.deficiency: No recovery phrase access after setup
falls_short.hush_requires: RecoveryPhraseVerify: user enters phrase in single textarea, validateMnemonic confirms validity. Phrase is NEVER stored.
Import from HTML file: file picker → parseHtmlBookmarks (lib/ call) → merge into tree → SAVE. Distinct from encrypted backup import (IMPORT_BACKUP message).

build_from_scratch: SETTINGS-001a: Wire all 10 NOT_IMPLEMENTED background handlers with real logic
SETTINGS-001b: components/screens/SettingsScreen.tsx — organized sections: Account, Import/Export, Data, About
components/settings/PasswordChangeForm.tsx — current + new + confirm flow via CHANGE_PASSWORD message
components/settings/RecoveryPhraseVerify.tsx — single textarea input, trim + lowercase + split on whitespace, validateMnemonic. Does NOT use MnemonicDisplay (that's for display, not input).
components/settings/ImportSection.tsx — 3 distinct flows: Chrome (IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS), HTML (parseHtmlBookmarks + SAVE), backup (IMPORT_BACKUP)
components/settings/ExportSection.tsx — EXPORT_BACKUP message. Disabled when session is locked.
components/settings/SetManagement.tsx — list/create/rename/delete sets via background messages
components/settings/ThemeToggle.tsx — dark/light/system. Reads/writes localStorage. App.tsx must also read on mount (coordinate with Module 10 dark mode init).

user_stories.title: Wire all 10 NOT_IMPLEMENTED background handlers
user_stories.description: Replace all 10 NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs in entrypoints/background.ts with real implementations using existing lib/ functions. This is backend-only — independently testable without any UI. Tests use the same mock-chrome pattern from Module 9.
steps: RED: Test: 'CHANGE_PASSWORD decrypts with old password and re-encrypts with new'
RED: Test: 'CHANGE_PASSWORD with wrong current password returns error'
RED: Test: 'UPDATE_AUTO_LOCK clears and recreates alarm with new minutes'
RED: Test: 'CREATE_SET creates set in manifest + saves empty tree encrypted with password'
RED: Test: 'RENAME_SET renames set in manifest'
RED: Test: 'DELETE_SET removes set from manifest + deletes storage key'
RED: Test: 'SWITCH_SET locks current + loads new set + updates session'
RED: Test: 'SWITCH_SET with wrong password returns INVALID_PASSWORD'
RED: Test: 'CLEAR_ALL with confirmation DELETE clears storage and locks session'
RED: Test: 'CLEAR_ALL rejects if confirmation is not DELETE'
RED: Test: 'IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS calls chrome.bookmarks.getTree, extracts result[0].children, converts via convertChromeBookmarks'
RED: Test: 'IMPORT_BACKUP calls importEncryptedBackup with blob + password'
RED: Test: 'EXPORT_BACKUP calls exportEncryptedBackup with cached tree + password'
RED: Test: 'EXPORT_BACKUP returns error when session is locked (no cached password)'
GREEN: Wire each handler in background.ts switch cases:

CHANGE_PASSWORD: loadSetData(activeSetId, msg.currentPassword) → saveSetData(activeSetId, plaintext, msg.newPassword) → update cached password → return success. Recovery blob unaffected (uses mnemonic, not user password).
UPDATE_AUTO_LOCK: chrome.alarms.clear(AUTO_LOCK_ALARM) → chrome.alarms.create(AUTO_LOCK_ALARM, { delayInMinutes: msg.minutes }) → return success
CREATE_SET: createSet(msg.name) → saveSetData(newSetId, JSON.stringify(createEmptyTree()), msg.password) → return { setId }
RENAME_SET: renameSet(msg.setId, msg.newName) → return success
DELETE_SET: deleteSet(msg.setId) → return success

SWITCH_SET: lock current (clear alarm, null tree/password) → setActiveSetId(msg.setId) → loadSetData(msg.setId, msg.password) → JSON.parse → cache tree + password → chrome.storage.session.set → create alarm → return SessionState
CLEAR_ALL: validate msg.confirmation === 'DELETE' → clearAll() → chrome.storage.session.clear() → null session → return success

IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS: chrome.bookmarks.getTree() → extract result[0]!.children (Chrome root node pattern — bookmarks bar, other, mobile are children of invisible root) → convertChromeBookmarks(children) → return { tree, stats }
IMPORT_BACKUP: importEncryptedBackup(msg.blob, msg.password) → return { tree }
EXPORT_BACKUP: validate cached password exists → exportEncryptedBackup(cachedTree, cachedPassword) → return { blob }
REFACTOR: Verify zero NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs remain (grep). Consider extracting handlers to separate file if background.ts exceeds 300 lines.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs replaced with real implementations
grep 'NOT_IMPLEMENTED' entrypoints/background.ts returns zero results
CHANGE_PASSWORD: decrypt old → re-encrypt new → save. Does NOT invalidate recovery blob.
CREATE_SET: creates empty tree, not null — JSON.stringify(createEmptyTree())
IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS: extracts result[0].children before passing to convertChromeBookmarks (Chrome root node pattern)
EXPORT_BACKUP: passes cachedTree (BookmarkTree object) directly to exportEncryptedBackup — no JSON.stringify (function handles serialization internally)
EXPORT_BACKUP: returns error when no cached password (session locked)
CLEAR_ALL: rejects unless confirmation === 'DELETE'
SWITCH_SET: full lock → unlock cycle with new set
All handlers use Result<T, E> pattern — check .success before proceeding
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/entrypoints/background.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit && grep -c NOT_IMPLEMENTED entrypoints/background.ts | grep '^0$'

user_stories.title: SettingsScreen shell with password change and recovery verification
user_stories.description: Create SettingsScreen with organized sections, password change flow, and recovery phrase verification. RecoveryPhraseVerify uses a single textarea (not MnemonicDisplay — that's for display, not input).
steps: RED: Test: 'SettingsScreen renders section headers'
RED: Test: 'back button navigates to tree screen'
RED: Test: 'PasswordChangeForm requires current, new, confirmation'
RED: Test: 'PasswordChangeForm rejects mismatched passwords'
RED: Test: 'PasswordChangeForm sends CHANGE_PASSWORD and waits for response'
RED: Test: 'RecoveryPhraseVerify has single textarea input'
RED: Test: 'RecoveryPhraseVerify trims, lowercases, splits on whitespace, validates via validateMnemonic'

GREEN: Create SettingsScreen with sections (Account, Import/Export, Data, About) and back nav. Create PasswordChangeForm (current → new → confirm → CHANGE_PASSWORD message → wait → success/error). Create RecoveryPhraseVerify: single textarea, on submit: trim + lowercase + split(/\s+/) → validateMnemonic(normalized) → show valid/invalid result. CANNOT display original phrase — never stored.
REFACTOR: Confirm RecoveryPhraseVerify does NOT import MnemonicDisplay
acceptance_criteria: SettingsScreen with organized sections and back navigation
PasswordChangeForm: current + new + confirm → CHANGE_PASSWORD → wait-for-response
RecoveryPhraseVerify: single textarea, trim + lowercase + split on whitespace → validateMnemonic
RecoveryPhraseVerify does NOT use MnemonicDisplay (that component is for display, not input)
Shows valid/invalid result. Cannot display the original phrase (by design).
components/settings/ directory is intentional — separates settings sub-components from shared components
All flows show loading states and error handling
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/components/ && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Import/Export: Chrome bookmarks, HTML file, encrypted backup
user_stories.description: Three distinct import flows + one export flow. Chrome import: IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS message → receive tree + stats → merge. HTML import: file picker → FileReader → parseHtmlBookmarks (lib/ call in UI — pure function, no browser API) → merge → SAVE. Backup import: IMPORT_BACKUP message. These are DIFFERENT flows — do not conflate.
steps: RED: Test: 'Chrome import button sends IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS and shows stats'
RED: Test: 'Chrome import merges result into current tree via SAVE'
RED: Test: 'HTML import opens file picker, reads file, calls parseHtmlBookmarks, merges via SAVE'
RED: Test: 'backup export sends EXPORT_BACKUP and triggers file download'
RED: Test: 'backup export button disabled when session is locked'
RED: Test: 'backup import sends IMPORT_BACKUP with blob + password (prompts for password)'
RED: Test: 'import shows stats after successful import'

GREEN: Create ImportSection with 3 flows: (1) Chrome: send IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS → receive { tree, stats } → append result.data.tree (the 'Imported' wrapper folder) as a single top-level folder into current tree root.children → SAVE. Note: convertChromeBookmarks wraps results in a folder named 'Imported' (bookmark-import.ts:52). (2) HTML: file input → FileReader.readAsText → parseHtmlBookmarks(html) → append result.data.tree (also an 'Imported' wrapper) to root.children → SAVE. parseHtmlBookmarks is a pure lib/ function safe to call from UI. (3) Backup: file input → read text → prompt for backup password → IMPORT_BACKUP { blob, password } → receive tree → replace current tree → SAVE. Create ExportSection: EXPORT_BACKUP → receive { blob } → create Blob → URL.createObjectURL → download link. Disable export button when !isUnlocked from SessionContext.
REFACTOR: Extract merge logic: append imported wrapper folder to current tree root.children
acceptance_criteria: 3 distinct import flows: Chrome (IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS), HTML (parseHtmlBookmarks + SAVE), backup (IMPORT_BACKUP)
Chrome and HTML import: append result.data.tree ('Imported' wrapper folder from bookmark-import.ts:52) as a single top-level folder in current tree root.children — do NOT flatten children
Backup import prompts for backup password separately and replaces entire tree
Export sends EXPORT_BACKUP → blob → download
Export button disabled when session is locked (no cached password)
Import stats displayed after success (from result.data.stats)
Merge strategy: result.data.tree is a Folder named 'Imported' containing all imported items — append it whole to root.children
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/components/settings/ && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Set management, auto-lock, theme, and clear data
user_stories.description: SetManagement (CREATE_SET, RENAME_SET, DELETE_SET), AutoLockConfig (UPDATE_AUTO_LOCK), ThemeToggle (localStorage — coordinate with Module 10 App.tsx dark mode init), ClearDataSection (CLEAR_ALL with double confirm).
steps: RED: Test: 'SetManagement lists sets from context'
RED: Test: 'create set sends CREATE_SET message'
RED: Test: 'delete set shows ConfirmDialog then sends DELETE_SET'
RED: Test: 'AutoLockConfig sends UPDATE_AUTO_LOCK with new minutes'
RED: Test: 'ClearDataSection requires double confirmation then sends CLEAR_ALL'
RED: Test: 'ThemeToggle cycles light/dark/system and persists to localStorage'

GREEN: Create SetManagement (list from SessionContext.sets, CREATE_SET/RENAME_SET/DELETE_SET messages. Cannot delete default set.). AutoLockConfig (input for minutes, UPDATE_AUTO_LOCK). ThemeToggle (3-way: light/dark/system, localStorage key 'hush-theme'. On mount reads localStorage and applies class. NOTE: App.tsx from Module 10 also needs to read this on mount to set initial theme — add a useTheme hook or inline the read in App.tsx. If Module 10 already handles dark mode via system preference, extend to also check localStorage.). ClearDataSection (ConfirmDialog → type 'DELETE' → CLEAR_ALL → lock → login).
REFACTOR: Consider extracting useTheme hook to hooks/ for shared popup/manager use
acceptance_criteria: SetManagement: list, create (CREATE_SET), rename (RENAME_SET), delete (DELETE_SET with ConfirmDialog)
Cannot delete default set
AutoLockConfig: UPDATE_AUTO_LOCK with new minutes value
ThemeToggle: light/dark/system. localStorage key 'hush-theme'. Applied on mount.
Theme initialization: App.tsx reads localStorage('hush-theme') on mount, falls back to system preference
ClearDataSection: double confirmation → CLEAR_ALL { confirmation: 'DELETE' } → lock → login
All via background messages. Zero direct storage calls (except localStorage for theme).
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/components/settings/ && npx tsc --noEmit

what_exists.popup: Modules 10-11 complete. App.tsx with state machine router, SessionContext, TreeContext. LoginScreen, SetupScreen, TreeScreen with full bookmark CRUD. All shared components: BookmarkTree, BookmarkItem, FolderItem, AddEditBookmarkDialog, AddFolderDialog, ConfirmDialog, FolderPicker, PasswordInput, SetPicker, MnemonicDisplay, ErrorBoundary.
what_exists.router: State machine with settings and import as valid screens from tree.
what_exists.background: Module 9. All 16 message types defined. 6 core handlers implemented. 10 handlers return NOT_IMPLEMENTED — SETTINGS-001a wires them up.
what_exists.dark_mode: Module 10 AUTH-004 implements dark mode via system preference. ThemeToggle in this module extends to localStorage-based user choice.

what_does_not_exist.settings: No SettingsScreen or any settings sub-components. No components/settings/ directory.
what_does_not_exist.import_export_ui: No ImportSection, ExportSection (lib/ functions exist but no UI).
what_does_not_exist.background_handlers: 10 message handlers in background.ts are NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.

forbidden_patterns: Hooks in lib/ (use hooks/ at root)

import_flows.chrome: IMPORT_CHROME_BOOKMARKS message → background calls chrome.bookmarks.getTree() → extracts result[0].children (Chrome root pattern) → convertChromeBookmarks → returns Result<{ tree, stats }>. UI appends result.data.tree ('Imported' wrapper folder) as single top-level folder to current root.children → SAVE.
import_flows.html: File picker → FileReader → parseHtmlBookmarks(html) — called DIRECTLY in UI (pure lib/ function, no browser API). Returns Result<{ tree, stats }>. UI appends result.data.tree ('Imported' wrapper folder) to root.children → SAVE.
import_flows.backup_import: File picker → read text → prompt password → IMPORT_BACKUP { blob, password } message to background → returns tree → replace current tree entirely → SAVE.
import_flows.backup_export: EXPORT_BACKUP message → background passes cachedTree (BookmarkTree object, NOT JSON string) + cachedPassword to exportEncryptedBackup → returns blob string → UI creates download. Disabled when locked.

context.import_merge_strategy: convertChromeBookmarks and parseHtmlBookmarks both return ImportResult where data.tree is a Folder named 'Imported' (bookmark-import.ts:52). The 'Imported' folder is appended WHOLE as a single top-level folder to current tree root.children. This is clean and reversible — user can delete the folder if unwanted. Backup import replaces the entire tree (different behavior).
context.password_change_flow: CHANGE_PASSWORD: background decrypts with old → re-encrypts with new → saves. Recovery blob unaffected (encrypted with mnemonic-derived password, NOT user password).
context.theme_coordination: ThemeToggle writes to localStorage('hush-theme'). App.tsx reads on mount: check localStorage first, fall back to prefers-color-scheme. Both popup and manager need this — consider useTheme hook in hooks/.
context.components_settings_directory: components/settings/ is intentional. Settings sub-components are numerous and only used by SettingsScreen. Keeping them separate from components/shared/ (which Module 13 reuses) is cleaner.
read_first: lib/password-sets.ts (saveSetData, loadSetData — string-based API, returns Result<T,E>)
lib/bookmark-import.ts (parseHtmlBookmarks, convertChromeBookmarks — both return ImportResult with 'Imported' wrapper folder)
lib/bookmark-backup.ts (exportEncryptedBackup takes BookmarkTree not string, importEncryptedBackup)

inputs.what_it_provides: ConfirmDialog, PasswordInput, dialogs. NOTE: MnemonicDisplay is NOT used by RecoveryPhraseVerify.
createSet, deleteSet, renameSet, listSets, saveSetData, loadSetData, getActiveSetId, setActiveSetId
convertChromeBookmarks, parseHtmlBookmarks — both return ImportResult = Result<{ tree: BookmarkTree, stats: ImportStats }>. tree is a wrapper Folder named 'Imported'.
exportEncryptedBackup(tree: BookmarkTree, password: string): Promise<string> — takes BookmarkTree object, NOT JSON string. importEncryptedBackup(blob: string, password: string)
Typed message sender for all background communication

known_risks.action: Chrome returns invisible root. Extract result[0].children before convertChromeBookmarks. Documented in SETTINGS-001a handler spec.

known_risks.issue: Recovery phrase can't be displayed — only verified
known_risks.action: RecoveryPhraseVerify with single textarea. NOT MnemonicDisplay.

known_risks.issue: background.ts may exceed 300 lines after 10 handlers
known_risks.action: Extract handler functions to background-handlers.ts if needed.
ThemeToggle writes localStorage. App.tsx reads on mount. Document in SETTINGS-003.
Disable export button when !isUnlocked. Background handler also validates cached password.
Takes BookmarkTree object, NOT JSON string. Function handles serialization internally. Documented in acceptance criteria.

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session. Wire background handlers FIRST (001a), then build UI (001b+). Test in wxt dev.
metadata.quality_gate: All 16 handlers functional. Zero NOT_IMPLEMENTED. Settings UI works. Password change works. Import/export works. Recovery verify uses textarea. Export disabled when locked.
metadata.human_review_required: Walk through: password change, Chrome import (verify 'Imported' folder preserved), HTML import, backup export/import, set management, clear data, theme toggle. Grep NOT_IMPLEMENTED — expect zero.

execution_rules.clarifying_questions: Encouraged. Key: Chrome root node extraction, theme localStorage coordination, handler file organization.
